title: Appendix Z: Resolving the Cosmological Lithium-7 Problem through the Universal Power Law β = 0.67: Mathematical Foundation and Experimental Verification via Anomalous Lithium Conductivity at High Pressure
description: We present a rigorous mathematical derivation of the universal conductivity power law σ ∝ (q-1)^{-β} with exponent β = 0.67, which follows from the Yakushev Unified Coordination Theory (YUCT). Using experimental data on the anomalous resistivity of lithium under shock compression up to 210 GPa (Fortov, Yakushev et al., 2001), we calibrate the model and demonstrate that the observed 15-fold increase in resistivity is a direct consequence of coordination effects. Three blind experimental predictions are proposed, including an isotope effect in conductivity σ_6/σ_7 = (7/6)^{0.74} ≈ 1.115, which provides a decisive test of the theory. The results close the loop from cosmological lithium to laboratory measurements and establish β = 0.67 as a new fundamental constant.

structural_topology.description: This directory contains the multilingual versions (EN, RU, DE, ZH, AR, JA, KO) of the paper 'Resolving the Cosmological Lithium-7 Problem through the Universal Power Law β = 0.67'. The root main file is the English version, while the other languages are full translations of the same content.

empirical_law_universality.beta_evidence: The exponent β = 2/3 is confirmed in lithium conductivity anomaly, cosmological lithium-7 problem, white dwarf redshift, metabolic scaling, and molecular vibration spectra.
empirical_law_universality.domains_covered: Condensed matter physics, nuclear astrophysics, gravity, biology

custom.historical_note: The key experiments on anomalous lithium conductivity were performed by the group led by V.V. Yakushev (Fortov et al. 1999-2002).

notes: This is the complete multilingual documentation of Appendix Z, version 1.0. Each language file (EN, RU, DE, ZH, AR, JA, KO) is a full translation of the entire text, including all mathematical derivations, tables, and references. The English version is the original reference. The Zenodo community for YUCT is 'yuct'.
